root_text.an3.18:1.1: “Sace vo, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
root_text.an3.18:1.3: Nanu tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ puṭṭhā aṭṭīyeyyātha harāyeyyātha jiguccheyyāthā”ti?
root_text.an3.18:1.5: “Iti kira tumhe, bhikkhave, dibbena āyunā aṭṭīyatha harāyatha jigucchatha, dibbena vaṇṇena dibbena sukhena dibbena yasena dibbenādhipateyyena aṭṭīyatha harāyatha jigucchatha;
root_text.an3.18:1.6: pageva kho pana, bhikkhave, tumhehi kāyaduccaritena aṭṭīyitabbaṁ harāyitabbaṁ jigucchitabbaṁ, vacīduccaritena … manoduccaritena aṭṭīyitabbaṁ harāyitabbaṁ jigucchitabban”ti.

translation_text.an3.18:1.1: “Mendicants, if wanderers who follow another religion were to ask you:
translation_text.an3.18:1.2: ‘Reverend, do you lead the spiritual life with the ascetic Gotama so that you can be reborn in the realm of the gods?’
translation_text.an3.18:1.3: Being questioned like this, wouldn’t you be horrified, repelled, and disgusted?”
translation_text.an3.18:1.5: “So it seems that you are horrified, repelled, and disgusted by heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, and sovereignty.
translation_text.an3.18:1.6: How much more then should you be horrified, embarrassed, and disgusted by bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.”
